description_long: This object is a robust, stone or concrete bollard with a tall, tapered obelisk-like central column rising from a wide, octagonal base. The base is thick and solid, providing stability, and is set atop a square platform that appears to be made of rough, aggregate concrete. Around the lower part of the column is a circular basin or collar, which may serve as a decorative or functional element. The column features metal plaques or plates on at least two sides, suggesting it may also serve a commemorative or informational purpose. The overall design is utilitarian yet monumental, suitable for demarcating boundaries or restricting vehicle access in urban plazas, parks, or historical sites.
description: A tall, obelisk-shaped stone bollard with an octagonal base, square platform, and metal plaques, featuring a circular basin around its lower column.
description_view_0: Front view shows the obelisk column, circular basin, and a visible opening or recess at the base, with the octagonal base and square platform clearly defined.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the thickness of the base and the depth of the circular basin, with no visible plaques or openings.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a metal plaque on the column and a solid, uninterrupted base, emphasizing the commemorative aspect.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the column, basin, and base, similar to view 1 but with a different angle on the base's geometry.
